findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/Decomposition.lean:80-84` proves only `congrArg φ (_root_.posPart_sub_negPart a)`; Mathlib’s source places `posPart_sub_negPart` in `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Order/Group/PosPart.lean` under the lattice ordered group sections, not a linear or commutative group section.
findings.fix: State the helper at the natural level, e.g. `[AddGroup α] [Lattice α] [AddLeftMono α] [AddGroup β]`, and derive the Weil-divisor degree wrappers from that or an equally general corollary.
findings.issue: `AddMonoidHom.map_posPart_sub_map_negPart` is over-specialized with `[AddCommGroup α] [LinearOrder α] [AddLeftMono α] [AddCommGroup β]`; the theorem only maps `posPart_sub_negPart` through an additive hom and does not need linearity or commutativity.

summary: One new generic helper is still stated below the natural Mathlib level: it assumes a linear order and commutativity even though the underlying positive/negative decomposition is a lattice-ordered group fact.
